nodes.x: After these things Jesus walked in Galilee: for he would not walk in Jewry, because the Jews sought to kill him.
Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews; but went thence unto a country near to the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim, and there continued with his disciples.
And therefore did the Jews persecute Jesus, and sought to slay him, because he had done these things on the sabbath day.
Then took they the body of Jesus, and wound it in linen clothes with the spices, as the manner of the Jews is to bury.
And when he heard of Jesus, he sent unto him the elders of the Jews, beseeching him that he would come and heal his servant.
Because that by reason of him many of the Jews went away, and believed on Jesus.
Now the Jews’ feast of tabernacles was at hand.
And they brought up the ark of the Lord, and the tabernacle of the congregation, and all the holy vessels that were in the tabernacle, even those did the priests and the Levites bring up.
¶ And they brought the tabernacle unto Moses, the tent, and all his furniture, his taches, his boards, his bars, and his pillars, and his sockets,

10 (return) [ This Jewish feast of tabernacles was imitated in several
heathen solemnities, as Spanheim here observes and proves. He also
further observes presently, what great regard many heathens had to the
monuments of their forefathers, as Nehemiah had here, sect. 6.]
Now when these things were thus ordained, the priests went always into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the service of God.
And they shall keep all the instruments of the tabernacle of the congregation, and the charge of the children of Israel, to do the service of the tabernacle.
His brethren therefore said unto him, Depart hence, and go into Judæa, that thy disciples also may see the works that thou doest.
Then after that saith he to his disciples, Let us go into Judæa again.
Then the disciples, every man according to his ability, determined to send relief unto the brethren which dwelt in Judæa:
And they said unto him, In Bethlehem of Judæa: for thus it is written by the prophet,
And he said unto the disciples, The days will come, when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man, and ye shall not see it.
¶ Then in the audience of all the people he said unto his disciples,
For there is no man that doeth any thing in secret, and he himself seeketh to be known openly. If thou do these things, shew thyself to the world.
Hast thou heard the secret of God? and dost thou restrain wisdom to thyself?
Can any hide himself in secret places that I shall not see him? saith the Lord. Do not I fill heaven and earth? saith the Lord.
But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast shut thy door, pray to thy Father who is in secret; and thy Father, who seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.
That thine alms may be in secret; and thy Father who seeth in secret, himself shall reward thee openly.
I have not spoken in secret, in a dark place of the earth: I said not unto the seed of Jacob, Seek ye me in vain: I the Lord speak righteousness, I declare things that are right.
For neither did his brethren believe in him.
For their heart was not right with him, neither were they steadfast in his covenant.
And when his brethren saw that their father loved him more than all his brethren, they hated him, and could not speak peaceably unto him.
But neither so did their witness agree together.
And Joseph knew his brethren, but they knew not him.
They did not believe what had been said concerning the resurrection of the dead, neither did they believe concerning the coming of Christ.
Then Jesus said unto them, My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready.
Then said Jesus unto them, Yet a little while am I with you, and then I go unto him that sent me.
Then said Jesus again unto them, I go my way, and ye shall seek me, and shall die in your sins: whither I go, ye cannot come.
And it shall come to pass that the time cometh, when the fulness of my gospel shall be preached unto them;
And he said unto them, Come again unto me after three days. And the people departed.
Then said Jesus unto them again, Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the door of the sheep.
The world cannot hate you; but me it hateth, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are evil.
If the world hate you, ye know that it hated me before it hated you.
But behold I say unto you, love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them who despitefully use you and persecute you;

therefore the world hate you! They may profess to love you, but without exception this profession is only to ensnare and ruin you, and then, mark it, laugh at your folly and gullibility. They are not of us, and
¶ But I say unto you which hear, Love your enemies, do good to them which hate you,
But I say unto you, Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you, and persecute you;
Go ye up unto this feast: I go not up yet unto this feast; for my time is not yet full come.
But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there be an uproar of the people.
But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there be an uproar among the people.
And ye shall offer up unto me no more the shedding of blood; yea, your sacrifices and your burnt offerings shall be done away, for I will accept none of your sacrifices and your burnt offerings.
¶ And thus shall ye eat it; with your loins girded, your shoes on your feet, and your staff in your hand; and ye shall eat it in haste: it is the Lord’s passover.
When he had said these words unto them, he abode still in Galilee.
¶ Now after two days he departed thence, and went into Galilee.
And it came to pass, that when Jesus had finished these sayings, he departed from Galilee, and came into the coasts of Judæa beyond Jordan;
He is not here, but is risen: remember how he spake unto you when he was yet in Galilee,
And when they had performed all things according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own city Nazareth.
They answered and said unto him, Art thou also of Galilee? Search, and look: for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.
¶ But when his brethren were gone up, then went he also up unto the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret.
And he also told him all the cause of his tarrying in his own kingdom, that he did not go unto his father to the feast which he had prepared.
And when he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem after the custom of the feast.
And he saith unto them, Draw out now, and bear unto the governor of the feast. And they bare it.
Then the Jews sought him at the feast, and said, Where is he?
Then sought they for Jesus, and spake among themselves, as they stood in the temple, What think ye, that he will not come to the feast?
And when they found him not, they turned back again to Jerusalem, seeking him.
After this there was a feast of the Jews; and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
Saying, Where is he that is born King of the Jews? for we have seen his star in the east, and are come to worship him.
And he came to Ephesus, and left them there: but he himself entered into the synagogue, and reasoned with the Jews.
And there was much murmuring among the people concerning him: for some said, He is a good man: others said, Nay; but he deceiveth the people.
For he was a good man, and full of the Holy Ghost and of faith: and much people was added unto the Lord.
And many more believed because of his own word;
And his father had not displeased him at any time in saying, Why hast thou done so? and he also was a very goodly man; and his mother bare him after Absalom.

And for the majesty that he gave him, all people, nations, and languages, trembled and feared before him: whom he would he slew; and whom he would he kept alive; and whom he would he set up; and whom he would he put down.
Howbeit no man spake openly of him for fear of the Jews.
These words spake his parents, because they feared the Jews: for the Jews had agreed already, that if any man did confess that he was Christ, he should be put out of the synagogue.
For surely he spake as touching all things concerning my people which are of the house of Israel; therefore it must needs be that he must speak also to the Gentiles.
Then said the Jews, Will he kill himself? because he saith, Whither I go, ye cannot come.
And many other things blasphemously spake they against him.
Then said the Jews, Behold how he loved him!
¶ Now about the midst of the feast Jesus went up into the temple, and taught.
And early in the morning he came again into the temple, and all the people came unto him; and he sat down, and taught them.
And it came to pass, that on one of those days, as he taught the people in the temple, and preached the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes came upon him with the elders,
¶ And they come again to Jerusalem: and as he was walking in the temple, there come to him the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders,
And Jesus went out, and departed from the temple: and his disciples came to him for to shew him the buildings of the temple.
And the Jews marvelled, saying, How knoweth this man letters, having never learned?
And when he had said these words, the Jews departed, and had great reasoning among themselves.
The man departed, and told the Jews that it was Jesus, which had made him whole.
And a superscription also was written over him in letters of Greek, and Latin, and Hebrew, This Is the King of the Jews.

"But the Jew knows none of these things. He is blinded with the
vain and delusive thought that his race, being the chosen of the
Lord, must forever remain in favor, whether they do iniquity or
not. Why! exclaims the Jew, we dare not for a moment look at things
in the light you suggest; it would disfranchise the whole nation
for eighteen centuries, and count them as aliens from the covenant
of…
Jesus answered them, and said, My doctrine is not mine, but his that sent me.
Jesus answered them, I told you, and ye believed not: the works that I do in my Father’s name, they bear witness of me.
Then I answered them, The word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
And Jesus answered and said unto them, I will also ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
And they answered Jesus, and said, We cannot tell. And he said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.
If any man will do his will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speak of myself.

Behold, this is the doctrine of Christ, and there will be no more doctrine given until after he shall manifest himself unto you in the flesh. And when he shall manifest himself unto you in the flesh, the things which he shall say unto you shall ye observe to do.
Behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, I will declare unto you my doctrine.
He that speaketh of himself seeketh his own glory: but he that seeketh his glory that sent him, the same is true, and no unrighteousness is in him.
He that walketh uprightly, and worketh righteousness, and speaketh the truth in his heart.
And I seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth.
He that speaketh truth sheweth forth righteousness: but a false witness deceit.
He that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul: he that keepeth understanding shall find good.
He that keepeth the commandment keepeth his own soul; but he that despiseth his ways shall die.
Did not Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keepeth the law? Why go ye about to kill me?
Behold, I say unto you that the law is fulfilled that was given unto Moses.
Remember ye the law of Moses, my servant, which I commanded unto him in Horeb for all Israel, with the statutes and judgments.
¶ Remember ye the law of Moses my servant, which I commanded unto him in Horeb for all Israel, with the statutes and judgments.

And again he said unto them: If ye teach the law of Moses why do ye not keep it? Why do ye set your hearts upon riches? Why do ye commit whoredoms and spend your strength with harlots, yea, and cause this people to commit sin, that the Lord has cause to send me to prophesy against this people, yea, even a great evil against this people?
And the Lord said unto Moses, How long refuse ye to keep my commandments and my laws?
The people answered and said, Thou hast a devil: who goeth about to kill thee?
Then answered the Jews, and said unto him, Say we not well that thou art a Samaritan, and hast a devil?
Jesus answered them, Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?
¶ And John answered him, saying, Master, we saw one casting out devils in thy name, and he followeth not us: and we forbad him, because he followeth not us.
Jesus answered, I have not a devil; but I honour my Father, and ye do dishonour me.
And said, O full of all subtilty and all mischief, thou child of the devil, thou enemy of all righteousness, wilt thou not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?
Jesus answered and said unto them, I have done one work, and ye all marvel.
¶ But Jesus answered them, My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.
Jesus answered and said unto them, This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.
Then said they unto him, What shall we do, that we might work the works of God?
Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision; (not because it is of Moses, but of the fathers;) and ye on the sabbath day circumcise a man.
If a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision, that the law of Moses should not be broken; are ye angry at me, because I have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day?

And it came to pass that there arose a great contention among the people concerning the law of circumcision, for the unbelieving husband was desirous that his children should be circumcised and become subject to the law of Moses, which law was fulfilled.
But there rose up certain of the sect of the Pharisees which believed, saying, That it was needful to circumcise them, and to command them to keep the law of Moses.

3. And it came to pass that there arose a great contention among
    the people concerning the law of circumcision, for the unbelieving
    husband was desirous that his children should be circumcised and
    become subject to the law of Moses, which law was fulfilled.
If a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision, that the law of Moses should not be broken; are ye angry at me, because I have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day?
Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision; (not because it is of Moses, but of the fathers;) and ye on the sabbath day circumcise a man.
Therefore if the uncircumcision keep the righteousness of the law, shall not his uncircumcision be counted for circumcision?
But when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto him, Behold, thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do upon the sabbath day.
Judge not according to the appearance, but judge righteous judgment.
Judge not, that ye be not judged.
The Lord shall judge the people: judge me, O Lord, according to my righteousness, and according to mine integrity that is in me.

The Righteous Judge By Elder Lynn G. Robbins Of the Presidency of the Seventy There is only one way to judge righteous judgment, as Jesus Christ does, and that is to be as He is. In His mortal life, Jesus Christ was a loving judge, uncommonly wise and patient. He is known in the scriptures as “the righteous judge” ( 2 Timothy 4:8 ; Moses 6:57 ), and His counsel to us is to also “judge righteous…
Then said some of them of Jerusalem, Is not this he, whom they seek to kill?

But all that heard him were amazed, and said; Is not this he that destroyed them which called on this name in Jerusalem, and came hither for that intent, that he might bring them bound unto the chief priests?
And when we heard these things, both we, and they of that place, besought him not to go up to Jerusalem.
And when he was come into Jerusalem, all the city was moved, saying, Who is this?
For he knew that Jerusalem must be destroyed, because of the wickedness of the people.
But, lo, he speaketh boldly, and they say nothing unto him. Do the rulers know indeed that this is the very Christ?
And behold, he that is faithful shall be made ruler over many things.
Of a truth I say unto you, that he will make him ruler over all that he hath.
And he that believeth not my words believeth not my disciples; and if it so be that I do not speak, judge ye; for ye shall know that it is I that speaketh, at the last day.

And he that will contend against the word of the Lord, let him be accursed; and he that shall deny these things, let him be accursed; for unto them will I show no greater things, saith Jesus Christ; for I am he who speaketh.
And when he shall prove himself faithful in all things that shall be entrusted unto his care, yea, even a few things, he shall be made ruler over many;
Howbeit we know this man whence he is: but when Christ cometh, no man knoweth whence he is.

No man can know Jesus the Christ except it be revealed from heaven to
him. 14:199.
And when the time cometh when all shall rise, then shall they know that God knoweth all the times which are appointed unto man.
When the Savior shall appear we shall see him as he is. We shall see that he is a man like ourselves.
We know that God spake unto Moses: as for this fellow, we know not from whence he is.
Then cried Jesus in the temple as he taught, saying, Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am: and I am not come of myself, but he that sent me is true, whom ye know not.
¶ Jesus cried and said, He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me.
Then said they unto him, Where is thy Father? Jesus answered, Ye neither know me, nor my Father: if ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also.
In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried unto my God: he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, even into his ears.
¶ Then said Jesus unto his disciples, If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
And saw him saying unto me, Make haste, and get thee quickly out of Jerusalem: for they will not receive thy testimony concerning me.
But I know him: for I am from him, and he hath sent me.
And he that seeth me seeth him that sent me.
But all these things will they do unto you for my name’s sake, because they know not him that sent me.
Him therefore I hope to send presently, so soon as I shall see how it will go with me.
And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he hath sent, him ye believe not.
He sent from above, he took me, he drew me out of many waters.
Then they sought to take him: but no man laid hands on him, because his hour was not yet come.
And some of them would have taken him; but no man laid hands on him.
¶ And they laid their hands on him, and took him.
Therefore they sought again to take him: but he escaped out of their hand,
And when they urged him till he was ashamed, he said, Send. They sent therefore fifty men; and they sought three days, but found him not.
And when he had taken him, and brought him to his mother, he sat on her knees till noon, and then died.
And many of the people believed on him, and said, When Christ cometh, will he do more miracles than these which this man hath done?
And the people saw it, and did witness of it, and were angry with him because of his power; and he did also do many more miracles, in the sight of the people, in the name of Jesus.
And who shall say that Jesus Christ did not do many mighty miracles? And there were many mighty miracles wrought by the hands of the apostles.
For this cause the people also met him, for that they heard that he had done this miracle.

And now it came to pass that according to our record, and we know our record to be true, for behold, it was a just man who did keep the record—for he truly did many miracles in the name of Jesus; and there was not any man who could do a miracle in the name of Jesus save he were cleansed every whit from his iniquity—
¶ Then gathered the chief priests and the Pharisees a council, and said, What do we? for this man doeth many miracles.
¶ The Pharisees heard that the people murmured such things concerning him; and the Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to take him.
And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things: and they derided him.
¶ And they send unto him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians, to catch him in his words.
And he charged them, saying, Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod.
And the Pharisees and scribes murmured, saying, This man receiveth sinners, and eateth with them.
And as he said these things unto them, the scribes and the Pharisees began to urge him vehemently, and to provoke him to speak of many things:
Then said Jesus unto them, Yet a little while am I with you, and then I go unto him that sent me.
Then said they unto him, Who art thou? And Jesus saith unto them, Even the same that I said unto you from the beginning.
Then said Jesus to them again, Peace be unto you: as my Father hath sent me, even so send I you.
Then said Jesus unto them, Be not afraid: go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.
Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.
What manner of saying is this that he said, Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come?
And ye shall seek me, and find me, when ye shall search for me with all your heart.
Then shall they call upon me, but I will not answer; they shall seek me early, but they shall not find me:
He that seeketh me early shall find me, and shall not be forsaken.
Then said the Jews among themselves, Whither will he go, that we shall not find him? will he go unto the dispersed among the Gentiles, and teach the Gentiles?
And he said unto me, Depart: for I will send thee far hence unto the Gentiles.

led away captive into all nations: and Jerusalem shall be trodden down of the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled.” How and in what way will the Lord fulfill the times of the Gentiles? And this is what our father meaneth; and he meaneth that it will not come to pass until after they are scattered by the Gentiles; and he meaneth that it shall come by way of the Gentiles, that the Lord may show his power unto the Gentiles, for the very cause that he shall be rejected of the Jews, or of the house of Israel.
Neither will he suffer that the Gentiles shall destroy the seed of thy brethren.
What manner of saying is this that he said, Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come?
Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.
And said unto him, Art thou he that should come, or do we look for another?
And he said, Therefore said I unto you, that no man can come unto me, except it were given unto him of my Father.
In the last day, that great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying, If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.
¶ After this, Jesus knowing that all things were now accomplished, that the scripture might be fulfilled, saith, I thirst.

And after that cometh the day of my power; then shall the poor, the lame, and the blind, and the deaf, come in unto the marriage of the Lamb, and partake of the supper of the Lord, prepared for the great day to come.
And it came to pass that he cried mightily unto the Lord all that day; and behold, the voice of the Lord came unto him, saying:
Now, when the multitude had all eaten and drunk, behold, they were filled with the Spirit; and they did cry out with one voice, and gave glory to Jesus, whom they both saw and heard.
He that believeth on me, as the scripture hath said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.
Jesus answered, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.
But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.
And worship him that made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the fountains of waters—
And I said unto him: Believest thou the scriptures? And he said, Yea.
(But this spake he of the Spirit, which they that believe on him should receive: for the Holy Ghost was not yet given; because that Jesus was not yet glorified.)
¶ And when Jesus had cried with a loud voice, he said, Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit: and having said thus, he gave up the ghost.
¶ Many of the people therefore, when they heard this saying, said, Of a truth this is the Prophet.
And to this agree the words of the prophets; as it is written,

“But there were false prophets also among the people, even as there shall be false teachers among you, who privily shall bring in damnable heresies, even denying the Lord that bought them, and bring upon themselves swift destruction. And many shall follow' their pernicious ways; by reason of whom the way of truth shall bo evil 11 apoken of.”
And many false prophets shall rise, and shall deceive many.
Then he said unto them, O fools, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken:
And many false prophets shall arise, and shall deceive many;
Others said, This is the Christ. But some said, Shall Christ come out of Galilee?
And the multitude said, This is Jesus the prophet of Nazareth of Galilee.
But go your way, tell his disciples and Peter that he goeth before you into Galilee: there shall ye see him, as he said unto you.
But after I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.
But after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.
And it came to pass in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized of John in Jordan.
Hath not the scripture said, That Christ cometh of the seed of David, and out of the town of Bethlehem, where David was?
And Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judæa, unto the city of David, which is called Bethlehem; (because he was of the house and lineage of David:)

Equally  definite  with  the  prophecies  declaring  that  the 
Messiah  would  be  born  in  the  lineage  of  David  are  the  pre- 
dictions that  fix  the  place  of  His  birth  at  Bethlehem,  a  small 
town  in  Judea.  There  seems  to  have  been  no  difference  of 
opinion  among  priests,  scribes,  or  rabbis  on  the  matter,  either 
before  or  since  the  great  event. …
And all the people were amazed, and said, Is not this the son of David?
And he said unto them, How say they that Christ is David’s son?
The words of the Preacher, the son of David, king in Jerusalem.
So there was a division among the people because of him.
And he increased his people greatly; and made them stronger than their enemies.
And the people saw them departing, and many knew him, and ran afoot thither out of all cities, and outwent them, and came together unto him.
For he was determined, because of the greatness of the number of his people, to overpower the Nephites and to bring them into bondage.

And he took the people, and divided them into three companies, and laid wait in the field, and looked, and, behold, the people were come forth out of the city; and he rose up against them, and smote them.
And some of them would have taken him; but no man laid hands on him.
Then they sought to take him: but no man laid hands on him, because his hour was not yet come.
And they stood forth and attempted to lay their hands on him; but he withstood them, and said unto them:
¶ Then came the officers to the chief priests and Pharisees; and they said unto them, Why have ye not brought him?
Now both the chief priests and the Pharisees had given a commandment, that, if any man knew where he were, he should shew it, that they might take him.
¶ The Pharisees heard that the people murmured such things concerning him; and the Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to take him.
And they which were sent were of the Pharisees.
The officers answered, Never man spake like this man.
Now no man at the table knew for what intent he spake this unto him.
For he spake, and it was done; he commanded, and it stood fast.
Then answered them the Pharisees, Are ye also deceived?
And Jesus answered and said unto them, Take heed that no man deceive you.
And Jesus answered, and said unto them: Take heed that no man deceive you;
Jesus answered them, Do ye now believe?
And Jesus answering them began to say, Take heed lest any man deceive you:
But some of them went their ways to the Pharisees, and told them what things Jesus had done.
Have any of the rulers or of the Pharisees believed on him?
¶ Nevertheless among the chief rulers also many believed on him; but because of the Pharisees they did not confess him, lest they should be put out of the synagogue:
There was a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews:
Then came together unto him the Pharisees, and certain of the scribes, which came from Jerusalem.
But this people who knoweth not the law are cursed.
Cursed be he that confirmeth not all the words of this law to do them. And all the people shall say, Amen.
Now we know that what things soever the law saith, it saith to them who are under the law: that every mouth may be stopped, and all the world may become guilty before God.
For as many as are of the works of the law are under the curse: for it is written, Cursed is every one that continueth not in all things which are written in the book of the law to do them.
And whosoever shall go to law with thee shall be cursed by the law.
Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree:
Nicodemus saith unto them, (he that came to Jesus by night, being one of them,)

"Now there was," we read, "a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a 
ruler of the Jews: the same came to Jesus by night, and said unto Him, 
Rabbi, we know that thou art a teacher come from God: for no man can do 
these miracles that thou doest, except God be with him. Jesus answered 
and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born
Nicodemus answered and said unto him, How can these things be?

the world; they may have studied mathematics, examined the physiology of the human system, and may have made themselves acquainted with geology, mineralogy, and the structure of the earth on which we live, and of the planetary system and the motion of worlds with which we are surrounded; they may have made themselves acquainted with history, geology, botany, law, physics, literature and theology,…

Son of God, believe He was a great inspired Teacher, and what He said was the word of life to the inhabitants of the earth. Nicodemus came to Jesus by night to enquire about the way of life. And “Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.” Nicodemus did not quite understand what was meant by being “born again,”…
And there came also Nicodemus, which at the first came to Jesus by night, and brought a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pound weight.
Doth our law judge any man, before it hear him, and know what he doeth?
Know ye not, brethren, (for I speak to them that know the law,) how that the law hath dominion over a man as long as he liveth?
For the Lord is our judge, the Lord is our lawgiver, the Lord is our king; he will save us.
And now behold, we will detect this man, and he shall confess his fault and make known unto us the true murderer of this judge.
If his children forsake my law, and walk not in my judgments;
Why seest thou this man, and hearest him revile against this people and against our law?
They answered and said unto him, Art thou also of Galilee? Search, and look: for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.
When he had said these words unto them, he abode still in Galilee.
¶ And they departed thence, and passed through Galilee; and he would not that any man should know it.
And he was seen many days of them which came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are his witnesses unto the people.
¶ And there arose not a prophet since in Israel like unto Moses, whom the Lord knew face to face,
And every man went unto his own house.
And all the people departed every man to his house: and David returned to bless his house.
And the man, the master of the house, went out unto them, and said unto them, Nay, my brethren, nay, I pray you, do not so wickedly; seeing that this man is come into mine house, do not this folly.
And they spake unto him the word of the Lord, and to all that were in his house.
Then the disciples went away again unto their own home.
And he burnt the house of the Lord, and the king’s house, and all the houses of Jerusalem, and every great man’s house burnt he with fire.
